01 — Background

An investor's eye, a builder's hands, an analyst's discipline.

Most agents learn the paperwork. You get one who learned the numbers first.

That experience is real, and it works in your favor. Running Haygood House Buyers meant owning every decision on an investment property end to end — pricing, budgeting, renovation, and resale. As an analytics specialist with Belmares Properties, the job was evaluating deals and pricing strategy for investors and negotiating lease terms to lift returns. Now, as your agent with MCL Realty, all of that goes to work for you — including creative financing on the transactions most agents would walk away from.

Add years on construction sites — superintendent work, reading blueprints, managing trades from groundbreaking to final inspection — and you get an agent who can tell you what a house is worth, what it will actually cost to fix, and how to finance the gap. That's a rarer combination than the market makes it sound.

Daniel Hernandez Haygood
02 — Portfolio

A few from the portfolio.

A selection of representative work across the San Antonio–Austin area — listings sold, new-construction closings on the buyer side, and investment renovations. Tap any image to enlarge.

03 — What I do

Built for the deals that need more than a lockbox.

A

Buying

Search, tour, and offer with someone running the math on every property — true cost of ownership, comps that hold up, and a negotiation strategy grounded in the numbers, not the asking price.

B

Selling

Pricing backed by real market analysis and a preparation plan that prioritizes the fixes that move value. You'll know exactly why we're asking what we're asking.

C

Investment analysis

Underwriting for buyers who think in returns: rental math, value-add scope, exit scenarios, and an honest read on whether a deal pencils before you ever write an offer.

D

Creative financing

When conventional terms don't fit the situation, I help structure alternatives — the kind of problem-solving that has moved tens of millions in complex transactions.

Why put an analyst in your corner?

Because a real estate decision is the largest financial decision most people make — and it deserves more than enthusiasm. The same discipline I used to evaluate deals for investors goes to work for you: clear assumptions, conservative numbers, and a recommendation I can defend line by line.

Underwriting
Every property gets the investor treatment, whether you're buying a first home or a fourth rental.
Construction fluency
Real site experience means realistic repair costs — no surprises after closing.
Deal structure
Financing creativity that keeps transactions alive when standard terms stall.
04 — How it goes

A straight line from first call to keys in hand.

01

Consult

We talk goals, budget, and timeline — and I tell you honestly what's realistic.

02

Analyze

I run the numbers on the market and the specific properties in play.

03

Strategy

We set price, terms, and a financing approach built around your situation.

04

Execute

Offers, negotiation, inspections, and contractors — managed and explained.

05

Close

To the finish line with no loose ends, and a relationship that outlasts the deal.
